title: Исправление ошибки: артикль перед гласной
description: Найдите и исправьте ошибку с артиклем.

content.incorrect_sentence: I have a dog. It is a animal.
content.correct_sentence: I have a dog. It is an animal.
content.explanation: Use 'an' before words that start with a vowel sound (a, e, i, o, u). 'Animal' starts with 'a', so we write 'an animal'.
options: I have a dog. It is a animal.
I have a dog. It is an animal.
I have a dog. It is the animal.
I have a dog. It is animal.

title: Исправление ошибки: прошедшее время
description: Найдите и исправьте ошибку со временем глагола.

content.incorrect_sentence: Yesterday I go to school.
content.correct_sentence: Yesterday I went to school.
content.explanation: The word 'yesterday' shows this happened in the past. 'Go' is present tense. The past tense of 'go' is 'went'.

title: Исправление ошибки: порядок слов
description: Найдите и исправьте ошибку в порядке слов.

content.incorrect_sentence: She likes very much coffee.
content.correct_sentence: She likes coffee very much.
content.explanation: In English, 'very much' comes after the object, not before it. The correct order is: verb + object + 'very much'.

title: Исправление ошибки: предлог времени
description: Найдите и исправьте ошибку с предлогом.

content.incorrect_sentence: I am at home in night.
content.correct_sentence: I am at home at night.
content.explanation: We use 'at' with night: 'at night'. We use 'in' with parts of the day like 'in the morning', 'in the evening', but 'at night' is the fixed expression.
options: I am at home in night.
I am at home at night.
I am at home on night.
I am at home by night.

title: Исправление ошибки: согласование во множественном числе
description: Найдите и исправьте ошибку согласования подлежащего и сказуемого.

content.incorrect_sentence: My friends is happy today.
content.correct_sentence: My friends are happy today.
content.explanation: 'Friends' is plural, so we use 'are' not 'is'. Rule: plural subjects take 'are'; singular subjects take 'is'.

title: Исправление ошибки: артикль с неисчисляемым существительным
title_en: Sentence Correction: Article with Uncountable Noun
description: Найдите и исправьте ошибку с артиклем.

content.incorrect_sentence: I drink a water every morning.
content.correct_sentence: I drink water every morning.
content.explanation: 'Water' is an uncountable noun and cannot be used with the indefinite article 'a/an'. Use it without an article when speaking in general.
options: I drink a water every morning.
I drink the water every morning.
I drink some a water every morning.

title: Исправление ошибки: настоящее совершённое с 'since'
description: Найдите и исправьте ошибку со временем глагола.

content.incorrect_sentence: I live here since 2015.
content.correct_sentence: I have lived here since 2015.
content.explanation: With 'since' (indicating a starting point that continues to now), use the present perfect: 'have/has + past participle'. Simple present 'live' is incorrect here.

title: Исправление ошибки: место наречия частотности
description: Найдите и исправьте ошибку в порядке слов.

content.incorrect_sentence: He always is tired after work.
content.correct_sentence: He is always tired after work.
content.explanation: Frequency adverbs like 'always', 'usually', 'often' go after the verb 'to be'. The correct order is: subject + be + adverb + adjective.
options: He always is tired after work.
He is always tired after work.
Always he is tired after work.
He is tired always after work.

title: Исправление ошибки: предлог с 'interested'
description: Найдите и исправьте ошибку с предлогом.

content.incorrect_sentence: She is interested on music.
content.correct_sentence: She is interested in music.
content.explanation: The fixed expression is 'interested in', not 'interested on'. We say: interested in something/someone.

title: Исправление ошибки: согласование с 'every'
description: Найдите и исправьте ошибку согласования подлежащего и сказуемого.

content.incorrect_sentence: Every student need to bring their book.
content.correct_sentence: Every student needs to bring their book.
content.explanation: 'Every' takes a singular verb even though it refers to many people. 'Every student' is grammatically singular, so the verb is 'needs', not 'need'.
options: Every student need to bring their book.
Every student needs to bring their book.
Every student are needing to bring their book.
Every students need to bring their book.

title: Исправление ошибки: условное предложение первого типа
description: Найдите и исправьте ошибку с временем в условном предложении.

content.incorrect_sentence: If I will study hard, I will pass the exam.
content.correct_sentence: If I study hard, I will pass the exam.
content.explanation: In first conditional sentences, the 'if' clause uses simple present tense, not 'will'. Only the main clause uses 'will'. Pattern: If + present simple, will + infinitive.

title: Исправление ошибки: артикль с абстрактным существительным
title_en: Sentence Correction: Article with Abstract Noun
description: Найдите и исправьте ошибку с артиклем.

content.incorrect_sentence: The life is beautiful and full of surprises.
content.correct_sentence: Life is beautiful and full of surprises.
content.explanation: Abstract nouns used in a general sense do not take an article in English. We say 'life is...' not 'the life is...' when speaking about life in general.

title: Исправление ошибки: предлог с длительностью
description: Найдите и исправьте ошибку с предлогом.

content.incorrect_sentence: She has been waiting since two hours.
content.correct_sentence: She has been waiting for two hours.
content.explanation: Use 'for' with a period of time (two hours, three days, a year). Use 'since' with a point in time (since Monday, since 2020). 'Two hours' is a duration, so 'for' is correct.

title: Исправление ошибки: порядок слов в косвенном вопросе
title_en: Sentence Correction: Word Order in Indirect Question
description: Найдите и исправьте ошибку в порядке слов.

content.incorrect_sentence: He told me where was the station.
content.correct_sentence: He told me where the station was.
content.explanation: In indirect (reported) questions, use statement word order: subject + verb. Do not use question word order (was the station). The question becomes: 'where the station was'.

title: Исправление ошибки: согласование с 'news'
description: Найдите и исправьте ошибку согласования.

content.incorrect_sentence: The news are very disturbing today.
content.correct_sentence: The news is very disturbing today.
content.explanation: 'News' looks plural because it ends in -s, but it is an uncountable noun and always takes a singular verb. Say 'the news is', not 'the news are'.

title: Исправление ошибки: прошедшее совершённое время
description: Найдите и исправьте ошибку со временем глагола.

content.incorrect_sentence: By the time she arrived, he has already left.
content.correct_sentence: By the time she arrived, he had already left.
content.explanation: When one past action happened before another past action, the earlier action uses past perfect (had + past participle). 'He had already left' before 'she arrived'.

title: Исправление ошибки: артикль с превосходной степенью
description: Найдите и исправьте ошибку с артиклем.

content.incorrect_sentence: She is a most talented musician I have ever heard.
content.correct_sentence: She is the most talented musician I have ever heard.
content.explanation: Superlatives always require the definite article 'the', not the indefinite 'a/an'. We say 'the most talented', 'the best', 'the largest', etc.

title: Исправление ошибки: предлог с 'succeed'
description: Найдите и исправьте ошибку с предлогом и формой глагола.

content.incorrect_sentence: She succeeded to pass the entrance exam.
content.correct_sentence: She succeeded in passing the entrance exam.
content.explanation: 'Succeed' is followed by 'in + gerund (-ing form)', not by 'to + infinitive'. The correct pattern is: succeed in doing something.

title: Исправление ошибки: инверсия после отрицательного наречия
title_en: Sentence Correction: Inversion after Negative Adverb
description: Найдите и исправьте ошибку в порядке слов.

content.incorrect_sentence: Rarely he speaks in public about his personal life.
content.correct_sentence: Rarely does he speak in public about his personal life.
content.explanation: When a negative or restrictive adverb (rarely, never, seldom, hardly) is placed at the start of a sentence for emphasis, the auxiliary verb and subject are inverted: Rarely does he...

title: Исправление ошибки: согласование с 'either...or'
description: Найдите и исправьте ошибку согласования.

content.incorrect_sentence: Either the manager or the staff are responsible for the delay.
content.correct_sentence: Either the manager or the staff is responsible for the delay.
content.explanation: With 'either...or' and 'neither...nor', the verb agrees with the subject closest to it. Here 'the staff' (closest) is a collective singular noun, so the verb is 'is'.

title: Исправление ошибки: сослагательное наклонение с 'wish'
description: Найдите и исправьте ошибку с временем глагола.

content.incorrect_sentence: I wish I know the answer to this question.
content.correct_sentence: I wish I knew the answer to this question.
content.explanation: After 'wish' expressing a desire about the present, use a past tense form (subjunctive). 'I wish I knew' means you currently don't know. Using simple present 'know' is incorrect.

title: Исправление ошибки: артикль с именем собственным
title_en: Sentence Correction: Article with Proper Name
description: Найдите и исправьте ошибку с артиклем.

content.incorrect_sentence: The Shakespeare wrote his tragedies during the reign of Queen Elizabeth I.
content.correct_sentence: Shakespeare wrote his tragedies during the reign of Queen Elizabeth I.
content.explanation: Personal names and author names do not take the definite article in English. We say 'Shakespeare', not 'the Shakespeare'. Articles with proper names signal a specific, commonly agreed upon work or entity (e.g., 'the Bard'), but not the person's name directly.

title: Исправление ошибки: предлог с 'composed'
description: Найдите и исправьте ошибку с предлогом.

content.incorrect_sentence: The committee is composed by representatives from twelve different countries.
content.correct_sentence: The committee is composed of representatives from twelve different countries.
content.explanation: The correct collocation is 'composed of', not 'composed by'. 'By' is used for passive agents (written by, created by). 'Composed of' means 'made up of' and describes the composition.

title: Исправление ошибки: инверсия после фронтированного отрицания
title_en: Sentence Correction: Inversion after Fronted Negative
description: Найдите и исправьте ошибку в порядке слов.

content.incorrect_sentence: Not until he read the report he realized the full extent of the problem.
content.correct_sentence: Not until he read the report did he realize the full extent of the problem.
content.explanation: When a fronted negative expression (Not until, Not once, Never, Only then) starts a main clause, subject-auxiliary inversion is required. The main clause becomes: 'did he realize', not 'he realized'.

title: Исправление ошибки: согласование с 'a number of'
title_en: Sentence Correction: Agreement with 'a number of'
description: Найдите и исправьте ошибку согласования.

content.incorrect_sentence: A number of students was absent from the lecture today.
content.correct_sentence: A number of students were absent from the lecture today.
content.explanation: 'A number of + plural noun' takes a plural verb. Contrast with 'the number of + plural noun', which takes a singular verb. 'A number of students were absent' vs 'The number of students was ten'.
